<system-reminder>
Instructions from: /home/uzer/baz/AGENTS.md
# BAZventures — Next.js + Supabase + Vercel

**Brand:** BAZventures · **Product:** the Hub · **Founder:** Brahim ZERBOUT  
**Design system source of truth:** `DESIGN-SYSTEM.md` (read it first).

## Project Setup
- GitHub repo: `baz-marketing-site` (remote: `origin`)
- Next.js 14 App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
- Supabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, Realtime, Storage, Edge Functions)
- Vercel (Analytics, Speed Insights, OG Images, Cron, Edge Runtime)
- CI/CD via GitHub Actions (test → deploy preview → deploy production)

## Design — Black & White Color System
- **B&W by default.** Color is a single expert-editable layer: `app/color-layer.css` (imported **last** in `app/layout.tsx` → always wins). Set `--seed-hue`/`--seed-sat` there to recolor the whole site (e.g. cyan `187/90%`, gold `42/85%`). One file, two numbers.
- **Dark mode only** — `ThemeProvider` forces `dark` (`forcedTheme="dark"`, `enableSystem={false}`). Background/text are seed-driven neutrals (currently greyscale).
- **4-layer CSS stack:** `globals.css` → `aether-theme.css` → `aether-monochrome.css` → `color-layer.css` (last wins). Full map in `DESIGN-SYSTEM.md`.
- **Fonts:** Outfit (display), Poppins (body), JetBrains Mono (code/metrics).
- **Square corners everywhere** (`* { border-radius: 0 }`); `rounded-full` only for pills/badges.
- **Tokens, not hexes:** never hardcode color in components — use semantic Tailwind classes (`text-foreground`, `bg-background`, `text-brand`, `bg-accent`, `border-border`) → tokens → `color-layer.css`.

## Brand Assets (B&W)
- **Favicon:** `public/favicon.svg` — B&W mark (dark squircle, light "B").
- **Logo (live):** a **text wordmark** rendered in `components/layout/Header.tsx` + `Footer.tsx` — a `bg-foreground` "B" square + "BAZventures" in Outfit. B&W, token-driven, auto-recolors via `--brand` when the seed is set.
- **Legacy logo SVGs** in `public/logo/` (`baz-mark*.svg`, `baz-wordmark*.svg`) are retained for the `/brandbook` reference page but are **not** used by the live header/footer.
- **OG images:** `/og`, `/stance-og`, `/case-studies-og`, `/methodology-og` — neutral palette, "BAZventures" wordmark.
- **No gold/accent color** anywhere on the public site until the expert sets the seed in `color-layer.css`.

## Key Scripts
- `npm run dev` — local dev (port 3000)
- `npm run build` — production build
- `npm test` — Playwright E2E tests
- `npm run supabase:types` — regenerate TS types from Supabase
- `npm run supabase:migrate` — push migrations to Supabase
- `npm run deploy` — deploy to Vercel

## Supabase
- Project: `uyqgmdrzyapbbvmaumvk.supabase.co`
- Migrations: `supabase/migrations/`
- Edge Functions: `supabase/functions/`
- Types: `lib/database.types.ts`
- Client: `lib/supabase-client.ts` (SSR + Admin + Anonymous)
- Realtime: `lib/realtime.ts` (client-side hooks)

## Vercel
- Project: `baz-marketing-site-y6bq` (team: brahimamirzerbout-9700s-projects)
- Analytics + Speed Insights in layout
- Dynamic OG images at `/og`
- Cron jobs: sitemap (daily), audit (weekly)
- Edge runtime: `/api/services`, `/api/health`

## CI/CD (GitHub Actions)
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
- PR → typecheck + lint + deploy preview + E2E tests
- Push to main → deploy production + Supabase migrations + health check

</system-reminder>

display.text:
# BAZventures — Agent Context

> Source of truth for the design system: **`DESIGN-SYSTEM.md`** (repo root).
> This file is quick orientation for any agent working in this repo.

## What this project is
- **Brand:** BAZventures (marketing agency). **Product:** the Hub (CRM/ops). **Founder:** Brahim ZERBOUT.
- Next.js 14 App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind v3 + Supabase + Vercel.
- Live: `github.com/brahimamirzerbout/baz-marketing-site` → Vercel project `baz-marketing-site-y6bq`.
- Public site is **black & white**; color is added later by a design expert via one file.

## Design system (current live state)
- **B&W by default.** Color = one file: `app/color-layer.css` (imported **last** in `app/layout.tsx` → always wins). Recolor by setting `--seed-hue`/`--seed-sat` (cyan `187/90%`, gold `42/85%`). State colors have their own hue/sat knobs in the same file.
- **Dark mode only** — `components/ui/ThemeProvider.tsx` uses `forcedTheme="dark"`, `enableSystem={false}`, `themes={["dark"]}`. Light tokens exist (`:root:not(.dark)`) but are dormant; see `DESIGN-SYSTEM.md` §6 before enabling light.
- **Fonts:** Outfit (display) · Poppins (body) · JetBrains Mono (code/metrics).
  - NOTE: an earlier direction used Fraunces/Inter — **superseded**. Do not reintroduce them.
- **Corners:** square (`* { border-radius: 0 }` in `globals.css`); `rounded-full` only for pills/badges/avatars.
  - NOTE: an earlier direction used Fibonacci radii — **superseded**. Do not reintroduce them.
- **4-layer CSS stack:** `globals.css` → `aether-theme.css` → `aether-monochrome.css` → `color-layer.css` (last wins).
- **Logo:** a text wordmark "BAZventures" rendered in `components/layout/Header.tsx` + `Footer.tsx` (a `bg-foreground` "B" square + wordmark). Legacy SVGs in `public/logo/` are kept for `/brandbook` only.
- **Rule:** tokens, not hexes. Use semantic Tailwind classes (`text-foreground`, `bg-background`, `text-brand`, `bg-accent`, `border-border`, `text-success`, …) → tokens → `color-layer.css`. Never hardcode color in components.

## Backend / data
- Database: better-sqlite3 (local) → Supabase (production). Types: `lib/database.types.ts`. Client: `lib/supabase-client.ts`.
- AI: Gemini (primary).
- Auth: JWT + cookies, no sessions.

## What NOT to do
- Do NOT hardcode color hexes in components — use tokens.
- Do NOT use Fraunces or Inter — live fonts are Outfit + Poppins + JetBrains Mono.
- Do NOT use Fibonacci radii — corners are square (only `rounded-full` for pills).
- Do NOT re-enable gold `#C8A55A` / `#E7C274` / orange `#F2572B` ad-hoc — all color flows through `color-layer.css`.
- Do NOT switch to Tailwind v4 `@theme` syntax (the project is on v3).
- Do NOT enable light mode piecemeal — the dark UI kit (glass, M2/M3 overlays) is deep; plan it per `DESIGN-SYSTEM.md` §6.
- Do NOT fabricate metrics — composite proof is flagged `[replace with real]` until real data lands.
- Do NOT rename the product "the Hub" or the social handles (`@bazagency`, `/baz-agency`, `baz.agency`) — founder confirmed those stay as-is.

## Key files
- `DESIGN-SYSTEM.md` — design system map (read this first).
- `AGENTS.md` — project setup, scripts, Supabase/Vercel/CI details.
- `app/color-layer.css` — the color control layer (expert edits here).
- `tailwind.config.ts` — tokens → Tailwind classes, type scale, square radii.
- `lib/site.ts` — brand name (BAZventures), product (the Hub), socials.
- `components/ui/ThemeProvider.tsx` — dark-only theme config.

<content>
1: # AGENTS.md — harness-memory
2: 
3: harness-memory — a **project memory layer for coding harnesses**: a memory/activation/
4: policy/dream engine that an coding agent's "adapter" plugs into so the agent gets
5: retrieved memories before model calls and captures evidence after tool calls.
6: 
7: ## Stack
8: - TypeScript, bundled with `tsup`; tests with `vitest`
9: - Local store (SQLite via migrations); embedding config for retrieval
10: 
11: ## Common commands
12: - `npm run build` — build (`tsup` + copy assets)
13: - `npm run test` — run tests (`vitest`)
14: - `npm run adapter:test` — exercise the adapter
15: - `npm run db:migrate` — run DB migrations
16: - `npm run init` — initialise a project
17: - Memory ops: `npm run memory:add|list|review|promote|reject|export|stats|why`
18: - Dream/eval: `npm run dream:run|extract|runs:list`, `npm run eval:baseline|memory`
19: - Policy: `npm run policy:check`
20: 
21: ## Adapter layer — the "pi-owned" path (important)
22: harness-memory talks to a coding harness via an **adapter** that implements the
23: `Adapter*` types in `src/adapters/types.ts` (`beforeModel`, `afterTool`, `sessionStart`,
24: `toolEvidence`, `policyCheck`, …). Today the only implementation is
25: `src/adapters/opencode-adapter.ts`, which depends on **`@opencode-ai/sdk`**
26: (opencode). That is the last opencode dependency in this project.
27: 
28: To make harness-memory **pi-owned**, implement a `PiAdapter` (`src/adapters/pi-adapter.ts`)
29: that maps pi's extension events onto the existing `Adapter*` contract:
30: - pi `before_agent_start`  → `beforeModel` (inject retrieved memories into the turn)
31: - pi `tool_call` / `tool_result` → `beforeTool` / `afterTool` (policy check + evidence capture)
32: - pi `session_start`        → `sessionStart`
33: - ship it as a pi extension (`pi.registerTool`/`pi.on`) in `src/plugin/pi-plugin.ts`
34:   (replacing `opencode-plugin.ts`)
35: 
36: Then remove `src/adapters/opencode-adapter.ts`, `src/plugin/opencode-plugin.ts`, the
37: `@opencode-ai/sdk` dependency, and the `opencode-adapter.test.ts`, and re-export
38: `PiAdapter` from `src/adapters/index.ts`. Until that swap is done, this project still
39: builds against opencode's SDK.
40: 
41: ## Notes for the coding agent (pi)
42: - pi is the coding agent for this project.
43: - A dependency-audit fix is staged on branch `pi-upgrade-20260705` (partial: vulns
44:   20→10, 4 crit/high remain that need a breaking `--force`). Review with
45:   `git diff master...pi-upgrade-20260705`; merge when satisfied.
46: - Run `npm run test` and `npm run adapter:test` after any adapter change.

(End of file - total 46 lines)
</content>

harness-memory — a **project memory layer for coding harnesses**: a memory/activation/
policy/dream engine that an coding agent's "adapter" plugs into so the agent gets
retrieved memories before model calls and captures evidence after tool calls.

## Stack
- TypeScript, bundled with `tsup`; tests with `vitest`
- Local store (SQLite via migrations); embedding config for retrieval

## Common commands
- `npm run build` — build (`tsup` + copy assets)
- `npm run test` — run tests (`vitest`)
- `npm run adapter:test` — exercise the adapter
- `npm run db:migrate` — run DB migrations
- `npm run init` — initialise a project
- Memory ops: `npm run memory:add|list|review|promote|reject|export|stats|why`
- Dream/eval: `npm run dream:run|extract|runs:list`, `npm run eval:baseline|memory`
- Policy: `npm run policy:check`

display.text:
# AGENTS.md — harness-memory

harness-memory — a **project memory layer for coding harnesses**: a memory/activation/
policy/dream engine that an coding agent's "adapter" plugs into so the agent gets
retrieved memories before model calls and captures evidence after tool calls.

## Stack
- TypeScript, bundled with `tsup`; tests with `vitest`
- Local store (SQLite via migrations); embedding config for retrieval

## Common commands
- `npm run build` — build (`tsup` + copy assets)
- `npm run test` — run tests (`vitest`)
- `npm run adapter:test` — exercise the adapter
- `npm run db:migrate` — run DB migrations
- `npm run init` — initialise a project
- Memory ops: `npm run memory:add|list|review|promote|reject|export|stats|why`
- Dream/eval: `npm run dream:run|extract|runs:list`, `npm run eval:baseline|memory`
- Policy: `npm run policy:check`

## Adapter layer — the "pi-owned" path (important)
harness-memory talks to a coding harness via an **adapter** that implements the
`Adapter*` types in `src/adapters/types.ts` (`beforeModel`, `afterTool`, `sessionStart`,
`toolEvidence`, `policyCheck`, …). Today the only implementation is
`src/adapters/opencode-adapter.ts`, which depends on **`@opencode-ai/sdk`**
(opencode). That is the last opencode dependency in this project.

To make harness-memory **pi-owned**, implement a `PiAdapter` (`src/adapters/pi-adapter.ts`)
that maps pi's extension events onto the existing `Adapter*` contract:
- pi `before_agent_start`  → `beforeModel` (inject retrieved memories into the turn)
- pi `tool_call` / `tool_result` → `beforeTool` / `afterTool` (policy check + evidence capture)
- pi `session_start`        → `sessionStart`
- ship it as a pi extension (`pi.registerTool`/`pi.on`) in `src/plugin/pi-plugin.ts`
  (replacing `opencode-plugin.ts`)

Then remove `src/adapters/opencode-adapter.ts`, `src/plugin/opencode-plugin.ts`, the
`@opencode-ai/sdk` dependency, and the `opencode-adapter.test.ts`, and re-export
`PiAdapter` from `src/adapters/index.ts`. Until that swap is done, this project still
builds against opencode's SDK.

## Notes for the coding agent (pi)
- pi is the coding agent for this project.
- A dependency-audit fix is staged on branch `pi-upgrade-20260705` (partial: vulns
  20→10, 4 crit/high remain that need a breaking `--force`). Review with
  `git diff master...pi-upgrade-20260705`; merge when satisfied.
- Run `npm run test` and `npm run adapter:test` after any adapter change.

1: # harness-memory
2: 
3: [![npm version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/harness-memory.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/harness-memory)

5: 
6: **Local-first project memory for AI coding assistants.**
7: 
8: harness-memory is an [OpenCode](https://opencode.ai) plugin that gives AI coding assistants persistent, reviewable project memory. It captures evidence from tool interactions and materializes memories through a multi-gate pipeline with human review.
9: 
10: **Evidence is not memory.** Conversation signals are captured automatically, but memories are curated through a deliberate lifecycle: Evidence → Signal Tags → Dream Consolidation → Candidate → Human Review → Active Memory.
11: 
12: ## Quick Start
13: 
14: ```bash
15: npx harness-memory install
16: ```
17: 
18: This single command creates the database, registers the plugin in `opencode.json`, installs slash commands, and imports existing `CLAUDE.md` / `.cursorrules` as baseline memories.
19: 
20: Restart OpenCode and you're ready.
21: 
22: ## How It Works
23: 
24: ### 1. Use Normally
25: 
26: Code with OpenCode as usual. The plugin silently injects relevant memories into the system prompt via a 4-layer activation engine.
27: 
28: ### 2. Evidence Capture (Automatic)
29: 
30: Every tool interaction generates evidence signals:
31: - Conversation is buffered and flushed to evidence storage
32: - Signal tags classify evidence (failure, decision, architecture, etc.)
33: - Tier classification separates high-signal from low-signal evidence
34: 
35: ### 3. Dream Consolidation
36: 
37: When the session goes idle, the plugin runs background consolidation:
38: - Reconciler checks existing memories first (reinforce > supersede > stale > create)
39: - LLM extraction proposes structured knowledge via OpenCode SDK
40: - Evidence flows through 7 states: `pending → retained → grouped → materialized/latent → consumed → discarded`
41: 
42: ### 4. Human Review
43: 
44: Extracted memories become candidates. Review them:
45: 
46: ```bash
47: npx harness-memory memory:review
48: ```
49: 
50: Or batch operations:
51: 
52: ```bash
53: npx harness-memory memory:promote --all --min-confidence 0.85
54: npx harness-memory memory:reject --all --max-confidence 0.5
55: ```
56: 
57: ### 5. Memory Injection
58: 
59: Approved memories are injected into every AI turn. The 4-layer activation engine selects the right memories for the right context:
60: 
61: | Layer | Purpose |
62: |-------|---------|
63: | **A: Baseline** | Always inject project fundamentals |
64: | **B: Startup** | First-turn boost via vector + lexical search |
65: | **C: Scoped** | File/path matching for current context |
66: | **D: Diversity** | Type balance + random discovery |
67: 
68: 4 activation modes route queries appropriately: `startup`, `default`, `temporal`, `cross_session`.
69: 
70: ## Why Not Just CLAUDE.md?
71: 
72: | | CLAUDE.md | harness-memory |
73: |--|-----------|----------------|
74: | **Storage** | Single file, manual edit | SQLite (WASM, zero native deps) |
75: | **Activation** | Dump everything every turn | 4-layer engine: right memory, right time |
76: | **Search** | None | Vector + lexical hybrid |
77: | **Lifecycle** | Edit the file yourself | Evidence → Dream → Human Review |
78: | **Token Cost** | Full dump every turn | 73% savings via selective injection |
79: | **Scaling** | Gets messy past 50 lines | Structured DB, handles hundreds |
80: 
81: ## Benchmark Results
82: 
83: Internal benchmarks using mock embeddings. Results indicate relative improvement direction, not absolute production performance.
84: 
85: ### Head-to-Head vs CLAUDE.md
86: 
87: 30 project rules across 12 coding tasks:
88: 
89: | Metric | CLAUDE.md | harness-memory |
90: |--------|-----------|----------------|
91: | Relevance Precision | 11.4% | 21.5% (1.9x) |
92: | Coverage | 100% | 48.6% |
93: | Tokens per task | 731 | 197 (73% savings) |
94: | Signal-to-Noise | 0.12 | 0.34 (2.7x) |
95: 
96: CLAUDE.md dumps everything (100% coverage, 11% relevant). harness-memory selectively injects (49% coverage, 22% relevant, 73% fewer tokens).
97:

102: | Metric | Score | What it measures |
103: |--------|-------|-----------------|
104: | False Positive Rate | 29% | Irrelevant memories in results |
105: | Topic Precision | 53% | Correct-topic memories in top results |
106: | Disambiguation | 50% | Distinguishing similar memories |
107: | Signal-to-Noise | 33% | Meaningful candidates from noisy evidence |
108: | Tag Accuracy | 57% | Regex signal tag correctness |
109: 
110: These scores reflect mock embedding limitations. Real embeddings are expected to improve retrieval metrics significantly.
111: 
112: ### Known Limitations
113: 
114: - **Early stage** (v0.5.0) — real-world validation in progress
115: - **Best for** compact-to-mid projects (up to 20 sessions)

149:   "plugin": ["harness-memory/plugin"]
150: }
151: ```
152: 
153: No additional configuration needed. Database auto-created at `.harness-memory/memory.sqlite`.
154: 
155: ## Tech Stack
156: 
157: - **TypeScript** (strict mode, ESM only)
158: - **sql.js** (SQLite via WASM, zero native dependencies)
159: - **@xenova/transformers** + e5-small (384d embeddings)
160: - **MiniSearch** (BM25 lexical search)
161: - **@opencode-ai/sdk** (LLM extraction)
162: - **vitest** (316 tests)

164: ## Contributing

display.text:
# harness-memory

[![npm version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/harness-memory.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/harness-memory)
[![license](https://img.shields.io/npm/l/harness-memory.svg)](https://github.com/smc2315/harness-memory/blob/master/LICENSE)

**Local-first project memory for AI coding assistants.**

harness-memory is an [OpenCode](https://opencode.ai) plugin that gives AI coding assistants persistent, reviewable project memory. It captures evidence from tool interactions and materializes memories through a multi-gate pipeline with human review.

**Evidence is not memory.** Conversation signals are captured automatically, but memories are curated through a deliberate lifecycle: Evidence → Signal Tags → Dream Consolidation → Candidate → Human Review → Active Memory.

## Quick Start

```bash
npx harness-memory install
```

This single command creates the database, registers the plugin in `opencode.json`, installs slash commands, and imports existing `CLAUDE.md` / `.cursorrules` as baseline memories.

Restart OpenCode and you're ready.

## How It Works

### 1. Use Normally

Code with OpenCode as usual. The plugin silently injects relevant memories into the system prompt via a 4-layer activation engine.

### 2. Evidence Capture (Automatic)

Every tool interaction generates evidence signals:
- Conversation is buffered and flushed to evidence storage
- Signal tags classify evidence (failure, decision, architecture, etc.)
- Tier classification separates high-signal from low-signal evidence

### 3. Dream Consolidation

When the session goes idle, the plugin runs background consolidation:
- Reconciler checks existing memories first (reinforce > supersede > stale > create)
- LLM extraction proposes structured knowledge via OpenCode SDK
- Evidence flows through 7 states: `pending → retained → grouped → materialized/latent → consumed → discarded`

### 4. Human Review

Extracted memories become candidates. Review them:

```bash
npx harness-memory memory:review
```

Or batch operations:

```bash
npx harness-memory memory:promote --all --min-confidence 0.85
npx harness-memory memory:reject --all --max-confidence 0.5
```

### 5. Memory Injection

Approved memories are injected into every AI turn. The 4-layer activation engine selects the right memories for the right context:

| Layer | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| **A: Baseline** | Always inject project fundamentals |
| **B: Startup** | First-turn boost via vector + lexical search |
| **C: Scoped** | File/path matching for current context |
| **D: Diversity** | Type balance + random discovery |

4 activation modes route queries appropriately: `startup`, `default`, `temporal`, `cross_session`.

## Why Not Just CLAUDE.md?

| | CLAUDE.md | harness-memory |
|--|-----------|----------------|
| **Storage** | Single file, manual edit | SQLite (WASM, zero native deps) |
| **Activation** | Dump everything every turn | 4-layer engine: right memory, right time |
| **Search** | None | Vector + lexical hybrid |
| **Lifecycle** | Edit the file yourself | Evidence → Dream → Human Review |
| **Token Cost** | Full dump every turn | 73% savings via selective injection |
| **Scaling** | Gets messy past 50 lines | Structured DB, handles hundreds |

## Benchmark Results

Internal benchmarks using mock embeddings. Results indicate relative improvement direction, not absolute production performance.

### Head-to-Head vs CLAUDE.md

30 project rules across 12 coding tasks:

| Metric | CLAUDE.md | harness-memory |
|--------|-----------|----------------|
| Relevance Precision | 11.4% | 21.5% (1.9x) |
| Coverage | 100% | 48.6% |
| Tokens per task | 731 | 197 (73% savings) |
| Signal-to-Noise | 0.12 | 0.34 (2.7x) |

CLAUDE.md dumps everything (100% coverage, 11% relevant). harness-memory selectively injects (49% coverage, 22% relevant, 73% fewer tokens).

### Stress Benchmarks (Realistic)

Adversarial tests exposing actual system limitations:

| Metric | Score | What it measures |
|--------|-------|-----------------|
| False Positive Rate | 29% | Irrelevant memories in results |
| Topic Precision | 53% | Correct-topic memories in top results |
| Disambiguation | 50% | Distinguishing similar memories |
| Signal-to-Noise | 33% | Meaningful candidates from noisy evidence |
| Tag Accuracy | 57% | Regex signal tag correctness |

These scores reflect mock embedding limitations. Real embeddings are expected to improve retrieval metrics significantly.

### Known Limitations

- **Early stage** (v0.5.0) — real-world validation in progress
- **Best for** compact-to-mid projects (up to 20 sessions)
- **Temporal reasoning** improved but not yet production-proven
- **Mock embeddings** in benchmarks; real performance will differ

## Memory Types

| Type | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `policy` | Rules and standards |
| `workflow` | Processes and conventions |
| `pitfall` | Known issues and gotchas |
| `architecture_constraint` | Design decisions and constraints |
| `decision` | Explicit choices and their rationale |

## CLI Reference

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npx harness-memory install` | Auto-setup (DB + plugin + slash commands + CLAUDE.md import) |
| `npx harness-memory memory:add --type <type> --summary "..."` | Add memory manually |
| `npx harness-memory memory:list` | List all memories |
| `npx harness-memory memory:promote --memory <id>` | Promote candidate to active |
| `npx harness-memory memory:reject --memory <id>` | Reject candidate |
| `npx harness-memory memory:review` | Interactive review session |
| `npx harness-memory memory:stats` | Usage statistics |
| `npx harness-memory memory:why` | Explain memory activation |
| `npx harness-memory memory:health-check` | System health diagnostics |
| `npx harness-memory dream:run` | Run dream consolidation |
| `npx harness-memory dream:extract` | LLM-based extraction |

## Configuration

```json
{
  "plugin": ["harness-memory/plugin"]
}
```

No additional configuration needed. Database auto-created at `.harness-memory/memory.sqlite`.

## Tech Stack

- **TypeScript** (strict mode, ESM only)
- **sql.js** (SQLite via WASM, zero native dependencies)
- **@xenova/transformers** + e5-small (384d embeddings)
- **MiniSearch** (BM25 lexical search)
- **@opencode-ai/sdk** (LLM extraction)
- **vitest** (316 tests)

## Contributing

Contributions and feedback welcome. Open an issue or PR on [GitHub](https://github.com/smc2315/harness-memory).

```bash
# Full test suite
npx vitest run

# Benchmark suite
npx vitest run test/benchmark/hm-*.test.ts

# Stress benchmarks
npx vitest run test/benchmark/hm-stress.test.ts
```

### Architecture

| Directory | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `src/activation/` | 4-layer activation engine, 4-mode routing |
| `src/dream/` | Evidence pipeline, signal tags, tier classification, reconciliation |
| `src/retrieval/` | Session summaries, query routing, hierarchical retrieval |
| `src/promotion/` | 5-gate auto-promoter, TTL, contradiction demotion |
| `src/security/` | Security scanner (Base64, Unicode confusable, injection detection) |
| `src/adapters/` | OpenCode adapter |
| `src/plugin/` | OpenCode plugin lifecycle hooks |

## License

MIT

<path>/home/uzer/harness-memory/src/plugin/pi-plugin.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * pi plugin for harness-memory.
3:  *
4:  * Bridges pi lifecycle events to the PiAdapter so that project memories are
5:  * injected before model calls, policy warnings are surfaced before tools, and
6:  * tool evidence is captured after tools — all automatically, inside pi.
7:  *
8:  * Loaded by pi when registered as a pi package/extension (see package.json
9:  * `"pi"` manifest, or `pi install npm:harness-memory`). Types for the pi
10:  * extension surface are defined inline to avoid a hard dependency on
11:  * `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent`; pi passes the real ExtensionAPI at runtime.
12:  *
13:  * Event mapping:
14:  *   pi `session_start`       → adapter.initializeSession
15:  *   pi `before_agent_start`  → adapter.beforeModel  (appends memory advisory to the system prompt)
16:  *   pi `tool_call`           → adapter.beforeTool   (policy warnings → ui.notify; never blocks)
17:  *   pi `tool_result`         → adapter.afterTool    (evidence capture + DB persist)
18:  *   pi `session_shutdown`    → persist DB
19:  *
20:  * Advanced features from the prior harness plugin (conversation buffer, session summary,
21:  * background dream extraction, embedding warmup, audit/file logging, review
22:  * digest, global memory tier) are intentionally deferred here — this is the
23:  * core, functional pi integration. Add them as pi event handlers on demand.
24:  */
25: 
26: import { existsSync, mkdirSync } from "node:fs";
27: import { dirname, resolve } from "node:path";
28: 
29: import { ActivationEngine } from "../activation";
30: import { PiAdapter } from "../adapters/pi-adapter";
31: import type { AdapterAfterToolOutput, AdapterModelRef } from "../adapters/types";
32: import { openSqlJsDatabase, saveSqlJsDatabase, runMigrations } from "../db";
33: import { DreamRepository } from "../dream";
34: import { MemoryRepository } from "../memory";
35: import { PolicyEngine, PolicyRuleRepository } from "../policy";
36: import type { Database as SqlJsDatabase } from "sql.js";
37: 
38: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
39: // Minimal inline pi extension surface (no hard runtime dependency)
40: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41: 
42: interface PiModelRef {
43:   provider: string;
44:   id: string;
45: }
46: 
47: interface PiExtensionContext {
48:   cwd: string;
49:   mode?: string;
50:   hasUI?: boolean;
51:   model?: PiModelRef | null;
52:   sessionManager?: { getSessionFile(): string | null };
53:   ui?: { notify(message: string, kind?: "info" | "warning" | "error"): void };
54: }
55: 
56: interface PiBeforeAgentStartEvent {
57:   prompt?: string;
58:   systemPrompt?: string;
59: }
60: 
61: interface PiToolCallEvent {
62:   toolName: string;
63:   toolCallId: string;
64:   input: unknown;
65: }
66: 
67: interface PiToolResultEvent {
68:   toolName: string;
69:   toolCallId: string;
70:   input?: unknown;
71:   content?: unknown[];
72:   details?: unknown;
73:   isError?: boolean;
74: }
75: 
76: interface PiSessionStartEvent {
77:   reason?: string;
78: }
79: 
80: interface PiApi {
81:   on(event: "session_start", handler: (e: PiSessionStartEvent, ctx: PiExtensionContext) => Promise<void> | void): void;
82:   on(
83:     event: "before_agent_start",
84:     handler: (e: PiBeforeAgentStartEvent, ctx: PiExtensionContext) => Promise<{ systemPrompt?: string } | void>,
85:   ): void;
86:   on(event: "tool_call", handler: (e: PiToolCallEvent, ctx: PiExtensionContext) => Promise<void>): void;
87:   on(event: "tool_result", handler: (e: PiToolResultEvent, ctx: PiExtensionContext) => Promise<void>): void;
88:   on(event: "session_shutdown", handler: (e: Record<string, unknown>, ctx: PiExtensionContext) => Promise<void> | void): void;
89: }
90: 
91: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
92: // Defaults
93: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
94: 
95: const DEFAULT_DB_RELATIVE_PATH = ".harness-memory/memory.sqlite";
96: const PLUGIN_LOG_PREFIX = "[harness-memory]";
97: 
98: function log(...args: unknown[]): void {
99:   console.log(PLUGIN_LOG_PREFIX, ...args);
100: }
101: 
102: function warn(...args: unknown[]): void {
103:   console.warn(PLUGIN_LOG_PREFIX, ...args);
104: }
105: 
106: /** Best-effort scope ref from a tool's args (looks for a path-like field). */
107: function extractScopeFromArgs(args: unknown): string | undefined {
108:   if (args === null || typeof args !== "object") return undefined;
109:   const obj = args as Record<string, unknown>;
110:   for (const key of ["path", "filePath", "file", "cwd", "directory"]) {
111:     const v = obj[key];
112:     if (typeof v === "string" && v.length > 0) return v.replace(/\\/g, "/");
113:   }
114:   return undefined;
115: }
116: 
117: /** Flatten pi tool-result content blocks into a single text blob. */
118: function flattenContent(content: unknown[] | undefined): string {
119:   if (!Array.isArray(content)) return "";
120:   const parts: string[] = [];
121:   for (const block of content) {
122:     if (block !== null && typeof block === "object" && "type" in block) {
123:       const b = block as Record<string, unknown>;
124:       if (b.type === "text" && typeof b.text === "string") parts.push(b.text);
125:     } else if (typeof block === "string") {
126:       parts.push(block);
127:     }
128:   }
129:   return parts.join("\n");
130: }
131: 
132: function modelRefFromCtx(ctx: PiExtensionContext): AdapterModelRef | undefined {
133:   const m = ctx.model;
134:   if (m === null || m === undefined) return undefined;
135:   return { providerID: m.provider, modelID: m.id };
136: }
137: 
138: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
139: // Plugin
140: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
141: 
142: export interface HarnessMemoryPiPluginOptions {
143:   /** Override the DB path (defaults to <cwd>/.harness-memory/memory.sqlite). */
144:   dbPath?: string;
145: }
146: 
147: export function createHarnessMemoryPiPlugin(
148:   options: HarnessMemoryPiPluginOptions = {},
149: ): (pi: PiApi) => void {
150:   return function harnessMemoryPiPlugin(pi: PiApi): void {
151:     let adapter: PiAdapter | null = null;
152:     let db: SqlJsDatabase | null = null;
153:     let dbPath = "";
154:     let currentSessionId: string | undefined;
155:     const pendingToolArgs = new Map<string, unknown>();
156: 
157:     async function ensureAdapter(ctx: PiExtensionContext): Promise<PiAdapter> {
158:       if (adapter !== null) return adapter;
159: 
160:       dbPath = options.dbPath ?? resolve(ctx.cwd, DEFAULT_DB_RELATIVE_PATH);
161:       if (!existsSync(dirname(dbPath))) mkdirSync(dirname(dbPath), { recursive: true });
162:       await runMigrations(dbPath, { log: () => {}, error: () => {} });
163: 
164:       db = await openSqlJsDatabase(dbPath);
165:       const memoryRepository = new MemoryRepository(db);
166:       const activationEngine = new ActivationEngine(memoryRepository);
167:       const policyRuleRepository = new PolicyRuleRepository(db);
168:       const policyEngine = new PolicyEngine(policyRuleRepository);
169:       const dreamRepository = new DreamRepository(db);
170: 
171:       adapter = new PiAdapter({ activationEngine, policyEngine, dreamRepository });
172:       log("Adapter initialized from", dbPath);
173:       return adapter;
174:     }
175: 
176:     function saveDb(): void {
177:       if (db !== null && dbPath.length > 0) {
178:         try {
179:           saveSqlJsDatabase(db, dbPath);
180:         } catch (error) {
181:           warn("Failed to persist DB:", error);
182:         }
183:       }
184:     }
185: 
186:     function sessionIdFor(ctx: PiExtensionContext): string {
187:       const file = ctx.sessionManager?.getSessionFile();
188:       return file ?? currentSessionId ?? "pi-session";
189:     }
190: 
191:     // ---- session_start: initialize the adapter session --------------------
192:     pi.on("session_start", async (_event, ctx) => {
193:       try {
194:         const adp = await ensureAdapter(ctx);
195:         currentSessionId = sessionIdFor(ctx);
196:         const model = modelRefFromCtx(ctx);
197:         adp.initializeSession({
198:           sessionID: currentSessionId,
199:           agent: "pi",
200:           model: model === undefined ? undefined : model,
201:         });
202:       } catch (error) {
203:         warn("session_start hook error:", error);
204:       }
205:     });
206: 
207:     // ---- before_agent_start: inject activated memories into the prompt ----
208:     pi.on("before_agent_start", async (event, ctx) => {
209:       try {
210:         const adp = await ensureAdapter(ctx);
211:         const sessionID = sessionIdFor(ctx);
212:         const model = modelRefFromCtx(ctx);
213:         const result = await adp.beforeModel({
214:           sessionID,
215:           model: model === undefined ? { providerID: "unknown", modelID: "unknown" } : model,
216:           messageText: event.prompt ?? "",
217:         });
218:         const advisory = result.advisoryText;
219:         if (advisory !== null && advisory.length > 0) {
220:           const base = event.systemPrompt ?? "";
221:           return { systemPrompt: base.length > 0 ? `${base}\n\n${advisory}` : advisory };
222:         }
223:       } catch (error) {
224:         warn("before_agent_start hook error:", error);
225:       }
226:       return undefined;
227:     });
228: 
229:     // ---- tool_call: policy warnings (non-blocking) + stash args -----------
230:     pi.on("tool_call", async (event, ctx) => {
231:       try {
232:         pendingToolArgs.set(event.toolCallId, event.input);
233:         const adp = await ensureAdapter(ctx);
234:         const sessionID = sessionIdFor(ctx);
235:         const result = await adp.beforeTool({
236:           sessionID,
237:           tool: event.toolName,
238:           callID: event.toolCallId,
239:           scopeRef: extractScopeFromArgs(event.input),
240:         });
241:         if (result.warningText !== null && ctx.hasUI !== false && ctx.ui) {
242:           ctx.ui.notify(result.warningText, "warning");
243:         }
244:       } catch (error) {
245:         warn("tool_call hook error:", error);
246:       }
247:     });
248: 
249:     // ---- tool_result: capture evidence + persist --------------------------
250:     pi.on("tool_result", async (event, ctx) => {
251:       try {
252:         const adp = await ensureAdapter(ctx);
253:         const sessionID = sessionIdFor(ctx);
254:         const args = pendingToolArgs.get(event.toolCallId) ?? event.input ?? {};
255:         pendingToolArgs.delete(event.toolCallId);
256:         const output: AdapterAfterToolOutput = {
257:           title: event.toolName,
258:           output: flattenContent(event.content),
259:           metadata: event.details,
260:         };
261:         await adp.afterTool(
262:           {
263:             sessionID,
264:             tool: event.toolName,
265:             callID: event.toolCallId,
266:             args,
267:             scopeRef: extractScopeFromArgs(args),
268:           },
269:           output,
270:         );
271:         saveDb();
272:       } catch (error) {
273:         warn("tool_result hook error:", error);
274:       }
275:     });
276: 
277:     // ---- session_shutdown: persist ---------------------------------------
278:     pi.on("session_shutdown", async () => {
279:       try {
280:         saveDb();
281:       } catch (error) {
282:         warn("session_shutdown hook error:", error);
283:       }
284:     });
285:   };
286: }
287: 
288: const harnessMemoryPiPlugin = createHarnessMemoryPiPlugin();
289: export default harnessMemoryPiPlugin;
290: export { harnessMemoryPiPlugin as HarnessMemoryPlugin };

(End of file - total 290 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
/**
 * pi plugin for harness-memory.
 *
 * Bridges pi lifecycle events to the PiAdapter so that project memories are
 * injected before model calls, policy warnings are surfaced before tools, and
 * tool evidence is captured after tools — all automatically, inside pi.
 *
 * Loaded by pi when registered as a pi package/extension (see package.json
 * `"pi"` manifest, or `pi install npm:harness-memory`). Types for the pi
 * extension surface are defined inline to avoid a hard dependency on
 * `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent`; pi passes the real ExtensionAPI at runtime.
 *
 * Event mapping:
 *   pi `session_start`       → adapter.initializeSession
 *   pi `before_agent_start`  → adapter.beforeModel  (appends memory advisory to the system prompt)
 *   pi `tool_call`           → adapter.beforeTool   (policy warnings → ui.notify; never blocks)
 *   pi `tool_result`         → adapter.afterTool    (evidence capture + DB persist)
 *   pi `session_shutdown`    → persist DB
 *
 * Advanced features from the prior harness plugin (conversation buffer, session summary,

65: function buildDreamTopicGuess(
66:   input: AdapterAfterToolInput,
67:   scopeRef: string,

69:   typeGuess: "policy" | "workflow" | "pitfall" | "architecture_constraint" | "decision"

71:   const normalizedScope = scopeRef.replace(/\\/g, "/");

102:   const workflowSignature = /passed|resolved|fixed|migrated/.test(text) ? "verified-flow" : input.tool;

136: function estimateDreamSalience(
137:   typeGuess: "policy" | "workflow" | "pitfall" | "architecture_constraint" | "decision",
138:   title: string,
139:   excerpt: string,
140:   toolName: string,
141:   relatedMemoryCount: number,
142:   conflictCount: number
143: ): number {
144:   let salience = 0.45;
145: 
146:   if (typeGuess === "pitfall") {
147:     salience += 0.25;
148:   }
149: 
150:   if (typeGuess === "policy" || typeGuess === "decision") {

154:   if (typeGuess === "architecture_constraint") {

167:     salience += 0.1;
168:   }

170:   if (toolName === "bash") {

307: /**
308:  * Format activated memories into prompt sections: static (baseline) and
309:  * dynamic (scoped/vector results). This separation enables LLM prompt
310:  * caching — the static section is identical across turns.
311:  */
312: function formatActivatedMemoryAdvisory(
313:   memories: readonly RankedMemory[]
314: ): string | null {
315:   const safeMemories = memories.filter((memory) => {
316:     const result = scanMemoryContent(memory.summary, memory.details);
317:     return result.safe;
318:   });
319: 
320:   if (safeMemories.length === 0) {
321:     return null;
322:   }
323: 
324:   const baseline = safeMemories.filter((m) => m.activationClass === "baseline");
325:   const dynamic = safeMemories.filter((m) => m.activationClass !== "baseline");
326: 
327:   const sections: string[] = [];
328: 
329:   // Static section — baseline memories (cacheable, identical across turns)
330:   if (baseline.length > 0) {
331:     sections.push("## Project Baseline");
332: 
333:     for (const memoryType of MEMORY_TYPE_ORDER) {
334:       const matching = baseline.filter((m) => m.type === memoryType);
335: 
336:       if (matching.length > 0) {
337:         sections.push("");
338:         sections.push(`### ${MEMORY_TYPE_PRESENTATION[memoryType].heading}`);
339: 
340:         for (const memory of matching) {
341:           const tier: DisclosureTier = "full";
342:           sections.push(formatMemoryLine(memory, tier));
343:         }
344:       }
345:     }
346:   }
347: 
348:   // Dynamic section — scoped/vector/event memories (varies per turn)
349:   if (dynamic.length > 0) {
350:     if (baseline.length > 0) {
351:       sections.push("");
352:     }
353: 
354:     sections.push("## Context Memories");
355: 
356:     for (const memoryType of MEMORY_TYPE_ORDER) {
357:       const matching = dynamic.filter((m) => m.type === memoryType);
358: 
359:       if (matching.length > 0) {
360:         sections.push("");
361:         sections.push(`### ${MEMORY_TYPE_PRESENTATION[memoryType].heading}`);
362: 
363:         for (const memory of matching) {
364:           const tier = getDisclosureTier(memory.rank, safeMemories.length);
365:           sections.push(formatMemoryLine(memory, tier));
366:         }
367:       }
368:     }
369:   }
370: 
371:   return sections.join("\n");
372: }
373: 
374: function formatPolicyWarning(warning: PolicyWarning): string {
375:   return `- [${warning.severity.toUpperCase()}] ${warning.ruleCode}: ${collapseWhitespace(warning.message)}`;
376: }
377: 
378: function formatPolicyWarnings(warnings: readonly PolicyWarning[]): string | null {
379:   if (warnings.length === 0) {
380:     return null;
381:   }
382: 
383:   return ["## Tool Warnings", "", ...warnings.map(formatPolicyWarning)].join("\n");
384: }
385: 
386: function serializeUnknownValue(value: unknown): string {
387:   if (value === undefined) {
388:     return "undefined";
389:   }
390: 
391:   if (typeof value === "string") {
392:     return value;
393:   }
394: 
395:   if (
396:     typeof value === "number" ||
397:     typeof value === "boolean" ||
398:     value === null
399:   ) {
400:     return String(value);
401:   }
402: 
403:   try {
404:     const serialized = JSON.stringify(value);
405:     return serialized ?? String(value);
406:   } catch {
407:     return String(value);
408:   }
409: }
410: 
411: function truncateText(value: string, maxChars: number): string {
412:   if (value.length <= maxChars) {
413:     return value;
414:   }
415: 
416:   const truncatedLength = Math.max(0, maxChars - 3);
417:   return `${value.slice(0, truncatedLength)}...`;
418: }
419: 
420: function buildToolEvidenceExcerpt(
421:   input: AdapterAfterToolInput,
422:   output: AdapterAfterToolOutput,
423:   maxChars: number
424: ): string {
425:   const lines = [
426:     `tool=${input.tool}`,
427:     `callID=${input.callID}`,
428:     `title=${output.title}`,
429:     `args=${serializeUnknownValue(input.args)}`,
430:     `output=${output.output}`,
431:   ];
432: 
433:   if (output.metadata !== undefined) {
434:     lines.push(`metadata=${serializeUnknownValue(output.metadata)}`);
435:   }
436: 
437:   return truncateText(lines.join("\n"), maxChars);
438: }
439: 
440: export class PiAdapter {
441:   readonly activationEngine: ActivationEngine;
442:   readonly policyEngine: PolicyEngine;
443:   readonly dreamRepository: DreamRepository | null;
444:   readonly defaultScopeRef: string;
445:   readonly evidenceExcerptMaxChars: number;
446:   readonly salienceBoundaryInterval: number;
447:   readonly salienceBoundaryBoost: number;
448: 
449:   private readonly sessions = new Map<string, AdapterSessionContext>();
450: 
451:   constructor(options: PiAdapterOptions) {
452:     this.activationEngine = options.activationEngine;
453:     this.policyEngine = options.policyEngine;
454:     this.dreamRepository = options.dreamRepository ?? null;
455:     this.defaultScopeRef = options.defaultScopeRef ?? DEFAULT_SCOPE_REF;
456:     this.evidenceExcerptMaxChars =
457:       options.evidenceExcerptMaxChars ?? DEFAULT_EVIDENCE_EXCERPT_MAX_CHARS;
458:     this.salienceBoundaryInterval =
459:       options.salienceBoundaryInterval ?? DEFAULT_SALIENCE_BOUNDARY_INTERVAL;
460:     this.salienceBoundaryBoost =
461:       options.salienceBoundaryBoost ?? DEFAULT_SALIENCE_BOUNDARY_BOOST;
462:   }
463: 
464:   getSession(sessionID: string): AdapterSessionContext | null {
465:     return this.sessions.get(sessionID) ?? null;
466:   }
467: 
468:   getOrCreateSession(sessionID: string): AdapterSessionContext {
469:     const existing = this.sessions.get(sessionID);
470: 
471:     if (existing !== undefined) {
472:       return existing;
473:     }
474: 
475:     const timestamp = nowIsoString();
476:     const session: AdapterSessionContext = {
477:       sessionID,
478:       createdAt: timestamp,
479:       lastUpdatedAt: timestamp,
480:       lastScopeRef: this.defaultScopeRef,
481:       metadata: {},
482:       lastBeforeModel: null,
483:       toolPolicyChecks: [],
484:       toolEvidence: [],
485:       beforeModelCount: 0,
486:       toolCallCount: 0,
487:     };
488: 
489:     this.sessions.set(sessionID, session);
490:     return session;
491:   }
492: 
493:   updateSessionMetadata(
494:     sessionID: string,
495:     metadata: AdapterSessionMetadata
496:   ): AdapterSessionContext {
497:     const session = this.getOrCreateSession(sessionID);
498:     session.metadata = mergeSessionMetadata(session.metadata, metadata);
499:     session.lastUpdatedAt = nowIsoString();
500:     return session;
501:   }
502: 
503:   initializeSession(input: AdapterSessionStartInput): AdapterSessionContext {
504:     return this.updateSessionMetadata(input.sessionID, input);
505:   }
506: 
507:   async beforeModel(input: AdapterBeforeModelInput): Promise<AdapterBeforeModelResult> {
508:     const session =
509:       input.sessionID === undefined ? null : this.getOrCreateSession(input.sessionID);
510:     const scopeRef = this.resolveScopeRef(input.scopeRef, session);
511:     const messageText = input.messageText ?? "";
512:     const queryMode = classifyQueryType(messageText);
513:     const activationMode =
514:       session !== null && session.toolCallCount === 0 && session.beforeModelCount === 0
515:         ? "startup"
516:         : queryMode;
517:     const activation = await this.activationEngine.activate({
518:       lifecycleTrigger: "before_model",
519:       scopeRef,
520:       types: input.types,
521:       queryTokens: input.queryTokens,
522:       repoFingerprint: input.repoFingerprint,
523:       maxMemories: input.maxMemories,
524:       maxPayloadBytes: input.maxPayloadBytes,
525:       activationMode,
526:     });
527:     const advisoryText = formatActivatedMemoryAdvisory(activation.activated);
528: 
529:     if (session !== null) {
530:       session.metadata = {
531:         ...session.metadata,
532:         model: {
533:           providerID: input.model.providerID,
534:           modelID: input.model.modelID,
535:         },
536:       };
537:       session.lastScopeRef = scopeRef;
538:       session.lastBeforeModel = {
539:         scopeRef,
540:         model: {
541:           providerID: input.model.providerID,
542:           modelID: input.model.modelID,
543:         },
544:         advisoryText,
545:         activation,
546:         createdAt: nowIsoString(),
547:       };
548:       session.beforeModelCount += 1;
549:       session.lastUpdatedAt = nowIsoString();
550:     }
551: 
552:     return {
553:       session,
554:       advisoryText,
555:       system: advisoryText === null ? [] : [advisoryText],
556:       activation,
557:     };
558:   }
559: 
560:   expandMemory(memoryId: string): string | null {
561:     const memory = this.activationEngine.repository.getById(memoryId);
562:     if (memory === null) {
563:       return null;
564:     }
565: 
566:     const scanResult = scanMemoryContent(memory.summary, memory.details);
567:     if (!scanResult.safe) {
568:       return null;
569:     }
570: 
571:     const presentation = MEMORY_TYPE_PRESENTATION[memory.type];
572:     if (presentation === undefined) {
573:       return null;
574:     }
575: 
576:     return [
577:       `## ${presentation.heading}: ${memory.summary}`,
578:       "",
579:       memory.details,
580:       "",
581:       `Type: ${memory.type} | Scope: ${memory.scopeGlob} | Confidence: ${memory.confidence}`,
582:     ].join("\n");
583:   }
584: 
585:   async beforeTool(input: AdapterBeforeToolInput): Promise<AdapterBeforeToolResult> {
586:     const session = this.getOrCreateSession(input.sessionID);
587:     const scopeRef = this.resolveScopeRef(input.scopeRef, session);
588:     const evaluation = this.policyEngine.evaluate({
589:       lifecycleTrigger: "before_tool",
590:       scopeRef,
591:     });
592:     const warningText = formatPolicyWarnings(evaluation.warnings);
593:     const activation = await this.activationEngine.activate({
594:       lifecycleTrigger: "before_tool",
595:       scopeRef,
596:       toolName: input.tool,
597:       activationMode: "default",
598:     });
599:     const advisoryText = formatActivatedMemoryAdvisory(activation.activated);
600:     const policyCheck: AdapterToolPolicyCheck = {
601:       toolName: input.tool,
602:       callID: input.callID,
603:       scopeRef,
604:       evaluatedAt: evaluation.evaluatedAt,
605:       warnings: evaluation.warnings,
606:       warningText,
607:     };
608: 
609:     session.toolPolicyChecks.push(policyCheck);
610:     session.lastScopeRef = scopeRef;
611:     session.lastUpdatedAt = nowIsoString();
612: 
613:     return {
614:       session,
615:       warnings: evaluation.warnings,
616:       warningText,
617:       advisoryText,
618:       activation,
619:       evaluatedAt: evaluation.evaluatedAt,
620:       blocked: false,
621:     };
622:   }
623: 
624:   async afterTool(
625:     input: AdapterAfterToolInput,
626:     output: AdapterAfterToolOutput
627:   ): Promise<AdapterAfterToolResult> {
628:     const session = this.getOrCreateSession(input.sessionID);
629:     const scopeRef = this.resolveScopeRef(input.scopeRef, session);
630:     const activation = await this.activationEngine.activate({
631:       lifecycleTrigger: "after_tool",
632:       scopeRef,
633:       types: input.types,
634:     });
635:     const excerpt = buildToolEvidenceExcerpt(
636:       input,
637:       output,
638:       this.evidenceExcerptMaxChars
639:     );
640:     const sourceRef = `${input.sessionID}:${input.callID}:${input.tool}`;
641:     const createdAt = nowIsoString();
642:     session.toolCallCount += 1;
643:     const salienceBoost =
644:       session.toolCallCount % this.salienceBoundaryInterval === 0
645:         ? this.salienceBoundaryBoost
646:         : 0;
647:     const createdEvidence = activation.activated.map((memory) =>
648:       this.activationEngine.repository.createEvidence({
649:         memoryId: memory.id,
650:         sourceKind: "session",
651:         sourceRef,
652:         excerpt,
653:         createdAt,
654:       })
655:     );
656: 
657:     if (this.dreamRepository !== null) {
658:       // Structural noise filter — NO regex, only length/emptiness checks
659:       if (!isEvidenceNoise(excerpt)) {
660:         const typeGuess = inferDreamTypeGuess(excerpt, output.title, input.tool);
661:         const topicGuess = buildDreamTopicGuess(input, scopeRef, excerpt, typeGuess);
662: 
663:         // Extract signal tags from evidence — these are HINTS, not gates
664:         const signalTags = extractEvidenceSignalTags(
665:           excerpt,
666:           JSON.stringify(input.args).toLowerCase()
667:         );
668: 
669:         // Build enriched metadata with signal tags and hint fields
670:         const evidenceMetadata: EvidenceMetadata = {
671:           ...(output.metadata !== undefined && typeof output.metadata === "object" && output.metadata !== null
672:             ? (output.metadata as Record<string, unknown>)
673:             : {}),
674:           signalTags,
675:           hintType: typeGuess,
676:           hintTopic: topicGuess,
677:         };
678: 
679:         this.dreamRepository.createEvidenceEvent({
680:           sessionId: input.sessionID,
681:           callId: input.callID,
682:           toolName: input.tool,
683:           scopeRef,
684:           sourceRef,
685:           title: output.title,
686:           excerpt,
687:           args: input.args,
688:           metadata: evidenceMetadata,
689:           // typeGuess and topicGuess still stored in columns for backward compat,
690:           // but the worker no longer uses them for gating or grouping decisions
691:           topicGuess,
692:           typeGuess,
693:           salience: estimateDreamSalience(
694:             typeGuess,
695:             output.title,
696:             excerpt,
697:             input.tool,
698:             activation.activated.length,
699:             activation.conflicts.length
700:           ),
701:           novelty: estimateDreamNovelty(
702:             typeGuess,
703:             activation.activated.length,
704:             activation.conflicts.length
705:           ),
706:           salienceBoost,
707:           contradictionSignal: activation.conflicts.length > 0,
708:           createdAt,
709:         });
710:       }
711:     }
712: 
713:     const evidenceCapture: AdapterToolEvidenceCapture = {
714:       toolName: input.tool,
715:       callID: input.callID,
716:       scopeRef,
717:       args: input.args,
718:       title: output.title,
719:       output: output.output,
720:       metadata: output.metadata,
721:       capturedAt: createdAt,
722:       activation,
723:       relatedMemoryIds: activation.activated.map((memory) => memory.id),
724:       evidence: createdEvidence,
725:       excerpt,
726:     };
727: 
728:     session.toolEvidence.push(evidenceCapture);
729:     session.lastScopeRef = scopeRef;
730:     session.lastUpdatedAt = createdAt;
731: 
732:     return {
733:       session,
734:       activation,
735:       relatedMemoryIds: evidenceCapture.relatedMemoryIds,
736:       createdEvidence,
737:       excerpt,
738:     };
739:   }
740: 
741:   private resolveScopeRef(
742:     scopeRef: string | undefined,
743:     session: AdapterSessionContext | null
744:   ): string {
745:     if (scopeRef !== undefined) {
746:       return scopeRef;
747:     }
748: 
749:     if (session !== null) {
750:       return session.lastScopeRef;
751:     }
752: 
753:     return this.defaultScopeRef;
754:   }
755: }

(End of file - total 755 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# Orchestrator

A meta-extension that curates pi's example extensions (`~/.pi/agent/extensions/examples/`)
into conflict-free **profiles** and activates the right bundle for the task.

## Why

The examples folder has 80+ extensions, many of which conflict (multiple footers,
editors, tool renderers…). The orchestrator picks combinations that compose and
**refuses** conflicting ones, so you get "perfect synergy" instead of a broken TUI.

## Profiles

| Profile | When | Extensions |
|---|---|---|
| `safe` | before destructive ops / sensitive repos | permission-gate, protected-paths, confirm-destructive, dirty-repo-guard |
| `git` | inside a git repo | git-checkpoint, dirty-repo-guard, auto-commit-on-exit |
| `ui` | richer interface | custom-header, custom-footer, titlebar-spinner, notify |
| `focus` | minimal/distraction-free | minimal-mode, hidden-thinking-label, working-indicator |
| `play` | long task, user waiting | snake, space-invaders, tic-tac-toe, notify |

display.text:
# Orchestrator

A meta-extension that curates pi's example extensions (`~/.pi/agent/extensions/examples/`)
into conflict-free **profiles** and activates the right bundle for the task.

## Why

The examples folder has 80+ extensions, many of which conflict (multiple footers,
editors, tool renderers…). The orchestrator picks combinations that compose and
**refuses** conflicting ones, so you get "perfect synergy" instead of a broken TUI.

## Profiles

| Profile | When | Extensions |
|---|---|---|
| `safe` | before destructive ops / sensitive repos | permission-gate, protected-paths, confirm-destructive, dirty-repo-guard |
| `git` | inside a git repo | git-checkpoint, dirty-repo-guard, auto-commit-on-exit |
| `ui` | richer interface | custom-header, custom-footer, titlebar-spinner, notify |
| `focus` | minimal/distraction-free | minimal-mode, hidden-thinking-label, working-indicator |
| `play` | long task, user waiting | snake, space-invaders, tic-tac-toe, notify |
| `review` | code review / long session | summarize, bookmark, trigger-compact |
| `power` | power-user model/tools/sessions | preset, tools, handoff, session-name |
| `plan` | exploring before changing | plan-mode |
| `context` | extra prompt context | claude-rules, prompt-customizer |

Profiles layer freely except for **singleton UI slots** (footer, header, editor,
tool rendering, working indicator, titlebar, persona, compaction handler, read
override). The orchestrator tells you exactly what conflicts.

## Usage

Agent-driven (the LLM decides "when appropriate"):

- the `orchestrate` tool — `action: enable|disable|status|list|reset`, with
  `profile` or `extension`. The orchestrator queues a `/reload` as a follow-up,
  so changes take effect on **subsequent** turns (not the current one).

User-driven:

- `/orchestrate` — interactive menu
- `/orchestrate-reload` — reload to apply changes (auto-queued)

## How it works

- Enable = copy `examples/<key>` → `extensions/<key>` (real file/dir so pi
  auto-discovers it after `/reload`).
- Disable = remove it, **only if the orchestrator enabled it** (tracked in
  `.orchestrator-state.json`). It never touches `baz/`, `examples/`,
  `orchestrator/`, or anything you added manually.
- Conflict check = no two members of the same singleton `group` active at once.

## Files

- `index.ts` — entry: registers tool, commands, session_start tip
- `catalog.ts` — extension metadata, profiles, conflict groups
- `manage.ts` — enable/disable/state/conflict logic (no pi imports → unit-testable)

## Coexists with `pi-failsafe`

In failsafe mode the whole `extensions/` dir is quarantined, including the
orchestrator and anything it enabled — so failsafe stays ollama-only. Nothing
to do; they're compatible.

state.output:
<path>/home/uzer/baz-agent-system/extensions/orchestrator/index.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: // Orchestrator: a meta-extension that curates pi's example extensions into
2: // conflict-free "profiles" and activates the right bundle for the task.
3: //
4: //   - /orchestrate            interactive menu (profiles + individual extensions)
5: //   - /orchestrate-reload      triggers ctx.reload() (queued as a follow-up by the tool)
6: //   - orchestrate (tool)       the agent calls this "when appropriate" to enable/disable
7: //
8: // Enable = copy examples/<key> -> extensions/<key>, then /reload. The new extensions
9: // become active on SUBSEQUENT turns (reload happens after the current turn finishes).
10: // The orchestrator refuses conflicting combos (singleton groups) -> "perfect synergy".
11: 
12: import type { ExtensionAPI } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent";
13: import { getAgentDir } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent";
14: import { Type } from "typebox";
15: import { StringEnum } from "@earendil-works/pi-ai";
16: import { join } from "node:path";
17: import { existsSync } from "node:fs";
18: import { createManager, type Result } from "./manage.ts";
19: import { PROFILES, CATALOG } from "./catalog.ts";
20: 
21: export default function (pi: ExtensionAPI) {
22:   const agentDir = getAgentDir();
23:   const extDir = join(agentDir, "extensions");
24:   const examplesDir = join(extDir, "examples");
25:   const stateFile = join(extDir, ".orchestrator-state.json");
26:   const haveExamples = existsSync(examplesDir);
27:   const mgr = createManager({ extDir, examplesDir, stateFile });
28: 
29:   // --- session_start: gentle, context-aware tip on a fresh startup (not on reload) ---
30:   pi.on("session_start", async (event, ctx) => {
31:     if (!haveExamples || event.reason !== "startup") return;
32:     try {
33:       const r = await pi.exec("git", ["rev-parse", "--is-inside-work-tree"], { cwd: ctx.cwd, timeout: 3000 });
34:       if (r.code === 0 && r.stdout.trim() === "true") {
35:         ctx.ui.notify("In a git repo — /orchestrate git (or ask the agent to enable the git profile).", "info");
36:       }
37:     } catch {
38:       /* ignore */
39:     }
40:   });
41: 
42:   // --- /orchestrate-reload: applies changes. Called as a follow-up so the current turn
43:   //     finishes first; the new extensions are active after reload. ---
44:   pi.registerCommand("orchestrate-reload", {
45:     description: "Reload pi to apply orchestrator extension changes",
46:     handler: async (_args, ctx) => {
47:       // ctx is ExtensionCommandContext, which exposes reload().
48:       await ctx.reload();
49:       return;
50:     },
51:   });
52: 
53:   // Helper to (maybe) queue a reload after a change.
54:   function maybeReload(changed: boolean) {
55:     if (changed) pi.sendUserMessage("/orchestrate-reload", { deliverAs: "followUp" });
56:   }
57: 
58:   // --- /orchestrate: interactive menu using built-in dialogs (robust, no custom TUI) ---
59:   pi.registerCommand("orchestrate", {
60:     description: "Enable/disable curated bundles of example pi extensions (profiles)",
61:     handler: async (_args, ctx) => {
62:       if (!haveExamples) {
63:         ctx.ui.notify(`Orchestrator: examples dir not found at ${examplesDir}. Sync the official examples first.`, "error");
64:         return;
65:       }
66:       const main = await ctx.ui.select("Orchestrator", [
67:         "Enable a profile",
68:         "Disable a profile",
69:         "Enable an extension",
70:         "Disable an extension",
71:         "Status",
72:         "Reset (disable all managed)",
73:       ]);
74:       if (!main) return;
75: 
76:       if (main === "Status") {
77:         ctx.ui.notify(mgr.status(), "info");
78:         return;
79:       }
80:       if (main === "Reset (disable all managed)") {
81:         const r = mgr.reset();
82:         ctx.ui.notify(r.message, r.changed ? "info" : "warning");
83:         maybeReload(r.changed);
84:         return;
85:       }
86:       if (main === "Enable a profile" || main === "Disable a profile") {
87:         const names = Object.keys(PROFILES);
88:         const labels = names.map((n) => `${n} — ${PROFILES[n].desc}`);
89:         const pick = await ctx.ui.select(main, labels);
90:         if (!pick) return;
91:         const prof = names[labels.indexOf(pick)];
92:         const r = main.startsWith("Enable") ? mgr.enableProfile(prof) : mgr.disableProfile(prof);
93:         ctx.ui.notify(r.message, r.ok ? "info" : "warning");
94:         maybeReload(r.changed);
95:         return;
96:       }
97:       if (main === "Enable an extension") {
98:         const labels = CATALOG.map((e) => `${e.key} — ${e.desc}${e.requiresSetup ? " (needs setup)" : ""}`);
99:         const pick = await ctx.ui.select("Enable extension", labels);
100:         if (!pick) return;
101:         const key = CATALOG[labels.indexOf(pick)].key;
102:         const r = mgr.enableKey(key);
103:         ctx.ui.notify(r.message, r.ok ? "info" : "warning");
104:         maybeReload(r.changed);
105:         return;
106:       }
107:       if (main === "Disable an extension") {
108:         const managed = mgr.readState().slice().sort();
109:         if (!managed.length) {
110:           ctx.ui.notify("Nothing managed by orchestrator to disable.", "warning");
111:           return;
112:         }
113:         const pick = await ctx.ui.select("Disable extension", managed);
114:         if (!pick) return;
115:         const r = mgr.disableKey(pick);
116:         ctx.ui.notify(r.message, r.ok ? "info" : "warning");
117:         maybeReload(r.changed);
118:         return;
119:       }
120:     },
121:   });
122: 
123:   // --- orchestrate tool (LLM-facing): the agent activates the right bundle ---
124:   pi.registerTool({
125:     name: "orchestrate",
126:     label: "Orchestrator",
127:     description:
128:       "Enable or disable curated bundles ('profiles') of pi example extensions so the right capabilities are active for the task. Changes apply after an automatic /reload, so they take effect on subsequent turns (not the current one). Use action=status to inspect active extensions and action=list to see available profiles. The orchestrator refuses conflicting combinations (singleton UI slots), keeping the setup conflict-free.",
129:     promptSnippet: "Enable/disable curated bundles of example pi extensions (profiles) matched to the task",
130:     promptGuidelines: [
131:       "Use orchestrate at the START of a task to activate the right example-extension bundle; changes take effect after a reload, on later turns — not mid-turn.",
132:       "Use orchestrate action=enable profile=safe before destructive operations (rm -rf, sudo, git push --force, dropping tables) or when working in sensitive repos.",
133:       "Use orchestrate action=enable profile=git when working inside a git repository (look for .git).",
134:       "Use orchestrate action=enable profile=review for code review or navigating a long session.",
135:       "Use orchestrate action=enable profile=play when a task will take a while and the user is waiting (adds games + finish notifications).",
136:       "Use orchestrate action=enable profile=focus when the user asks for a minimal/distraction-free interface; profile=ui when they want a richer interface.",
137:       "Use orchestrate action=enable profile=plan when exploring a codebase before making changes; profile=power for power-user model/tools/session management.",
138:       "Call orchestrate action=status before enabling to avoid conflicts; if a profile is refused due to a singleton conflict, disable the conflicting profile/extension first.",
139:       "Profiles layer freely except for singleton UI slots (footer, header, editor, tool rendering, working indicator, titlebar). The orchestrator tells you exactly what conflicts.",
140:     ],
141:     parameters: Type.Object({
142:       action: StringEnum(["enable", "disable", "status", "list", "reset"] as const),
143:       profile: Type.Optional(Type.String({ description: "Profile name (enable/disable a whole bundle). See action=list." })),
144:       extension: Type.Optional(
145:         Type.String({ description: "Single example key, e.g. 'snake.ts' or 'plan-mode' (enable/disable one extension)" }),
146:       ),
147:     }),
148:     async execute(_toolCallId, params, _signal, _onUpdate, _ctx) {
149:       if (!haveExamples) {
150:         return {
151:           content: [{ type: "text", text: `Orchestrator: examples dir not found at ${examplesDir}. Sync the official examples first.` }],
152:           details: { ok: false },
153:         };
154:       }
155:       const action = params.action as "enable" | "disable" | "status" | "list" | "reset";
156: 
157:       if (action === "status") {
158:         return { content: [{ type: "text", text: mgr.status() }], details: { ok: true } };
159:       }
160:       if (action === "list") {
161:         const lines = ["Profiles (curated, conflict-free bundles):"];
162:         for (const [n, p] of Object.entries(PROFILES)) {
163:           lines.push(`- ${n}: ${p.desc}`);
164:           lines.push(`    when: ${p.when}`);
165:           lines.push(`    keys: ${p.keys.join(", ")}`);
166:         }
167:         lines.push("", `Cataloged extensions: ${CATALOG.length} (use action=enable extension=<key> for a single one).`);
168:         lines.push(`Examples dir: ${examplesDir}`);
169:         return { content: [{ type: "text", text: lines.join("\n") }], details: { ok: true } };
170:       }
171: 
172:       let r: Result;
173:       if (action === "enable") {
174:         if (params.profile) r = mgr.enableProfile(params.profile);
175:         else if (params.extension) r = mgr.enableKey(params.extension);
176:         else r = { ok: false, message: "enable requires 'profile' or 'extension'", changed: false };
177:       } else if (action === "disable") {
178:         if (params.profile) r = mgr.disableProfile(params.profile);
179:         else if (params.extension) r = mgr.disableKey(params.extension);
180:         else r = { ok: false, message: "disable requires 'profile' or 'extension'", changed: false };
181:       } else {
182:         r = mgr.reset();
183:       }
184: 
185:       const followup =
186:         r.changed
187:           ? "\n\nA /orchestrate-reload has been queued as a follow-up. The new extensions become active on subsequent turns."
188:           : "";
189:       if (r.changed) pi.sendUserMessage("/orchestrate-reload", { deliverAs: "followUp" });
190:       return {
191:         content: [{ type: "text", text: `${r.message}${followup}` }],
192:         details: { ok: r.ok, changed: r.changed },
193:       };
194:     },
195:   });
196: }

(End of file - total 196 lines)
</content>

1: // Catalog of pi example extensions + curated, conflict-free profiles.
2: // `key` is the on-disk name under ~/.pi/agent/extensions/ (e.g. "snake.ts" or "plan-mode").
3: // Extensions sharing a `group` are singletons: only one may be active at a time
4: // (the orchestrator refuses to enable a second member of the same group).
5: 
6: export type CatalogEntry = {
7:   key: string;
8:   desc: string;
9:   group?: string; // singleton conflict group
10:   requiresSetup?: boolean; // external deps/config needed -> orchestrator won't auto-enable
11:   note?: string;
12: };
13: 
14: export type Profile = {
15:   desc: string;
16:   when: string; // when the agent should enable it
17:   keys: string[]; // example keys to enable
18: };
19: 
20: export const PROFILES: Record<string, Profile> = {
21:   safe: {
22:     desc: "Safety gates: confirm dangerous ops, protect sensitive paths, guard dirty repos",
23:     when: "before destructive commands (rm -rf, sudo, git push --force, dropping tables) or work in sensitive repos",

state.output:
<path>/home/uzer/baz-agent-system/extensions/orchestrator/manage.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: // Pure filesystem-level management of enabled example extensions.
2: // Enable = copy the example from <examplesDir>/<key> to <extDir>/<key> (real file/dir,
3: //   so pi's auto-discovery picks it up after /reload).
4: // Disable = remove <extDir>/<key>, but ONLY if it's in our managed state file
5: //   (never touch baz/, orchestrator/, examples/, or user-added files).
6: // Conflict check = no two members of the same singleton `group` active at once.
7: //
8: // No pi imports here -> unit-testable in isolation.
9: 
10: import { cpSync, rmSync, existsSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, mkdirSync } from "node:fs";
11: import { join } from "node:path";
12: import { CATALOG, PROFILES, type CatalogEntry } from "./catalog.ts";
13: 
14: export type Result = {
15:   ok: boolean;
16:   message: string;
17:   changed: boolean;
18:   enabled?: string[];
19:   conflicts?: string[];
20: };
21: 
22: export function createManager(opts: { extDir: string; examplesDir: string; stateFile: string }) {
23:   const { extDir, examplesDir, stateFile } = opts;
24: 
25:   const byKey = new Map<string, CatalogEntry>();
26:   for (const e of CATALOG) byKey.set(e.key, e);
27: 
28:   function readState(): string[] {
29:     try {
30:       const v = JSON.parse(readFileSync(stateFile, "utf8"));
31:       return Array.isArray(v) ? v as string[] : [];
32:     } catch {
33:       return [];
34:     }
35:   }
36: 
37:   function writeState(names: string[]) {
38:     mkdirSync(extDir, { recursive: true });
39:     writeFileSync(stateFile, JSON.stringify([...new Set(names)].sort(), null, 2));
40:   }
41: 
42:   // All example keys present on disk (managed by us OR added manually), plus any
43:   // managed keys we believe are enabled (even if a scan would miss them).
44:   function presentKeys(): Set<string> {
45:     const out = new Set<string>();
46:     for (const k of byKey.keys()) if (existsSync(join(extDir, k))) out.add(k);
47:     for (const k of readState()) if (existsSync(join(extDir, k))) out.add(k);
48:     return out;
49:   }
50: 
51:   function conflictsFor(key: string): string[] {
52:     const e = byKey.get(key);
53:     if (!e?.group) return [];
54:     const present = presentKeys();
55:     const others: string[] = [];
56:     for (const k of present) {
57:       if (k === key) continue;
58:       const oe = byKey.get(k);
59:       if (oe?.group === e.group) others.push(k);
60:     }
61:     return others;
62:   }
63: 
64:   function enableKey(key: string): Result {
65:     const src = join(examplesDir, key);
66:     const dst = join(extDir, key);
67:     if (!existsSync(src)) {
68:       return { ok: false, message: `No such example: '${key}' (not found under ${examplesDir}). Try action=list.`, changed: false };
69:     }
70:     if (existsSync(dst)) {
71:       // Already present (managed or manual). Make sure it's tracked.
72:       const st = readState();
73:       if (!st.includes(key)) { st.push(key); writeState(st); }
74:       return { ok: true, message: `'${key}' is already enabled`, changed: false };
75:     }
76:     const e = byKey.get(key);
77:     if (e?.requiresSetup) {
78:       return { ok: false, message: `'${key}' requires setup (${e.note ?? "external dependencies/config"}). Install/configure it, then enable manually.`, changed: false };
79:     }
80:     const conflicts = conflictsFor(key);
81:     if (conflicts.length) {
82:       return {
83:         ok: false,
84:         message: `Conflict: '${key}' is in singleton group '${e?.group}', already held by ${conflicts.join(", ")}. Disable that first (orchestrate disable) or pick a different one.`,
85:         changed: false,
86:         conflicts,
87:       };
88:     }
89:     try {
90:       cpSync(src, dst, { recursive: true });
91:     } catch (err) {
92:       return { ok: false, message: `Failed to copy '${key}': ${(err as Error).message}`, changed: false };
93:     }
94:     const st = readState();
95:     if (!st.includes(key)) { st.push(key); writeState(st); }
96:     return { ok: true, message: `Enabled '${key}'${e?.note ? ` (${e.note})` : ""}`, changed: true, enabled: [key] };
97:   }
98: 
99:   function disableKey(key: string): Result {
100:     const dst = join(extDir, key);
101:     const st = readState();
102:     if (!st.includes(key)) {
103:       if (existsSync(dst)) {
104:         return { ok: false, message: `'${key}' is present but not managed by orchestrator. Remove it manually if desired.`, changed: false };
105:       }
106:       return { ok: true, message: `'${key}' is not enabled`, changed: false };
107:     }
108:     try {
109:       rmSync(dst, { recursive: true, force: true });
110:     } catch (err) {
111:       return { ok: false, message: `Failed to remove '${key}': ${(err as Error).message}`, changed: false };
112:     }
113:     writeState(st.filter((k) => k !== key));
114:     return { ok: true, message: `Disabled '${key}'`, changed: true };
115:   }
116: 
117:   function enableProfile(profile: string): Result {
118:     const p = PROFILES[profile];
119:     if (!p) {
120:       return { ok: false, message: `No such profile: '${profile}'. Available: ${Object.keys(PROFILES).join(", ")}`, changed: false };
121:     }
122:     const enabled: string[] = [];
123:     const skipped: string[] = [];
124:     const failed: string[] = [];
125:     for (const k of p.keys) {
126:       const r = enableKey(k);
127:       if (r.changed) enabled.push(k);
128:       else if (r.ok) skipped.push(k);
129:       else failed.push(`${k}: ${r.message}`);
130:     }
131:     const parts: string[] = [];
132:     if (enabled.length) parts.push(`Enabled: ${enabled.join(", ")}`);
133:     if (skipped.length) parts.push(`Already active: ${skipped.join(", ")}`);
134:     if (failed.length) parts.push(`Skipped: ${failed.join("; ")}`);
135:     return {
136:       ok: failed.length === 0,
137:       message: `Profile '${profile}' — ${p.desc}\n${parts.join(" | ")}`,
138:       changed: enabled.length > 0,
139:       enabled,
140:     };
141:   }
142: 
143:   function disableProfile(profile: string): Result {
144:     const p = PROFILES[profile];
145:     if (!p) return { ok: false, message: `No such profile: '${profile}'`, changed: false };
146:     const disabled: string[] = [];
147:     for (const k of p.keys) {
148:       const r = disableKey(k);
149:       if (r.changed) disabled.push(k);
150:     }
151:     return {
152:       ok: true,
153:       message: `Disabled profile '${profile}': ${disabled.length ? disabled.join(", ") : "nothing to disable"}`,
154:       changed: disabled.length > 0,
155:     };
156:   }
157: 
158:   function reset(): Result {
159:     const st = readState();
160:     const removed: string[] = [];
161:     for (const k of st) {
162:       try {
163:         rmSync(join(extDir, k), { recursive: true, force: true });
164:         removed.push(k);
165:       } catch {
166:         /* ignore */
167:       }
168:     }
169:     writeState([]);
170:     return {
171:       ok: true,
172:       message: `Reset: removed ${removed.length ? removed.join(", ") : "nothing (no managed extensions)"}`,
173:       changed: removed.length > 0,
174:     };
175:   }
176: 
177:   function status(): string {
178:     const managed = readState().slice().sort();
179:     const present = [...presentKeys()].sort();
180:     const groups = new Map<string, string[]>();
181:     for (const k of present) {
182:       const e = byKey.get(k);
183:       if (e?.group) groups.set(e.group, [...(groups.get(e.group) ?? []), k]);
184:     }
185:     const conflictWarnings: string[] = [];
186:     for (const [g, ks] of groups) if (ks.length > 1) conflictWarnings.push(`group '${g}' has multiple active: ${ks.join(", ")}`);
187:     const lines: string[] = [];
188:     lines.push(`Managed by orchestrator: ${managed.length ? managed.join(", ") : "(none)"}`);
189:     lines.push(`Present on disk: ${present.length ? present.join(", ") : "(none)"}`);
190:     if (conflictWarnings.length) lines.push(`CONFLICTS: ${conflictWarnings.join("; ")}`);
191:     lines.push(`Profiles: ${Object.keys(PROFILES).join(", ")}`);
192:     return lines.join("\n");
193:   }
194: 
195:   return { readState, presentKeys, conflictsFor, enableKey, disableKey, enableProfile, disableProfile, reset, status, byKey, PROFILES };
196: }

(End of file - total 196 lines)
</content>
